content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach maintains genuine VIEW throughout. Pace is consistently slower than the client's — when she speeds into intellectualization (turn 5) or performs an agenda (turn 11), the coach does not match the rush but instead returns to body/present-moment with short, spacious utterances. There is no fixing or partner-picking engagement, no validation of the disdain for the ex, no diagnostic move into attachment-style language even when the client offers that frame on a platter (turn 13). The questions are short, one at a time, and emerge from what is actually happening in the room (the move from tightness to anger to research; the word 'broken'), which signals real wonder rather than scripted probing. Turn 12 ('No. But I notice you just called yourself broken') is undefended and impartial — the coach refuses to play the expert role she's being offered without making a thing of the refusal. Silence is held in turn 11 such that the client notices she's filling it. Vulnerability-as-first-person-disclosure is not explicit, but the coach's willingness to not-know and not-supply-the-answer functions as an undefended stance. No filler validation, no preacher mode, no agenda pressure detectable beneath the questions.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opens with a simple reflection and one question; doesn't collude with the curse/victim framing."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Brings attention to body without forcing — genuine curiosity about where the feeling lives."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the speed-up without judgment; tracks the actual phenomenon in front of him rather than the content she's offering."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Short, undefended counter to her productivity frame; no lecture, just an opening."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Slows pace further, invites the bigness without naming it for her — impartial wonder."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses the expert role cleanly ('No.') and reflects her own word back; no agenda, no fixing."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Holds the moment instead of capitalizing on it — stays with what is, doesn't push for insight."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["looking-for-whats-wrong", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["i-dont-buy-your-story", "if-i-were-inherently-good"],
  "notes": "Stance is clean even though some signature moves (deconstruct-the-victim, the adored-openly question) haven't yet appeared — that's a Methodology Fidelity concern, not a stance concern. The coach is clearly in VIEW."
}
